websites.title: GS Communication
websites.desc:
Designed and developed this website for the engineering firm GS Communication, giving them their first web presence. The backend is developed with Python,Flask, and Jinja2. The frontend is designed using HTML5, CSS3 and Bootstrap.

Note: This site is no longer active but the code is still accessible through the link above.

websites.title: M&G Wedding
websites.desc:
I designed and developed this responsive website for the wedding of my brother and his fiance. The site incorporates Bootstrap, HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript,jQuery and KnockoutJS. It is a single page application (SPA). To minimize the costs for the bride and groom, the site was hosted on Github Pages using a custom domain name. The RSVPs were handled using Google Docs services.

Note: This site is still accessible through the links above but it is no longer being maintained.

websites.title: Body Evolution
websites.desc:
I maintained and updated this website based on the information provided by the site designer. Through this WordPress powered website, I gained hands-on experience working with the Genesis Framework, some PHP and WordPress as a Content Management System.

Note: I am no longer maintaining this site.

websites.title: Catechism Class
websites.desc: I develop new code and update existing JavaScript/jQuery to improve client-facing features. I also review and update JavaScript/jQuery to current standards, fix bugs and improve speed and efficiency. I collaborate with other team members to maintain and update the website that uses PHP and MySQL in the backend.

websites.title: Code and Light
websites.desc:
This website has been updated and recreated using ReactJS. Tachyons.io is used to style the page instead of Bootstrap.

It was originally built using Python and Flask to serve the backend and Jinja2 was used for creating the HTML template. Bootstrap, along with custom CSS3 and JavaScript/jQuery are used to handle the frontend.

websites.title: Pacific Pathology, Inc.
websites.desc:
I developed the first iteration of this website. This resulted in an increase of clients and it extended their client-based outside of San Diego County.

Note: This site is now managed by another person.
